rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (codex hit a rate limit, human just said 'lgtm'), so both correctly land on APPROVE with no missed issues—recall is trivially satisfied for both. Y's hunk-by-hunk validation cites test file line numbers (2533, 2546, 2615) that exactly match the diff's line arithmetic, making its grounding independently verifiable from the given diff; X cites test line numbers (2638/2651/2720) that don't reconcile with the shown hunks, and its PR-body repro detail ('/v1/audio/voices') and upstream PR number are unverifiable extras. Both give clear, line-referenced reasoning and correctly explain the AttributeError mechanism via the base()/instrumentator path, but Y's precise, self-consistent citations edge out X's slightly looser sourcing.
